content: Marcus is a 42-year-old architect working at a firm in Portland, Oregon. He specializes in sustainable building design and has been in the field for 15 years.
Marcus lives in Portland with his wife Sarah and their two kids — Liam (12) and Mia (8). They have a house in the Alberta Arts District.
Marcus is a serious runner. He completed the Portland Marathon last year and trains four times a week. He also enjoys grilling steaks on weekends — he considers himself a barbecue enthusiast.
Marcus studied architecture at the University of Oregon, where he earned his M.Arch degree. He's proud of his thesis on biophilic design principles.
Marcus mentioned he's been feeling burned out with traditional architecture work. He's been exploring UX design as a potential career shift, taking online courses in the evenings.
Marcus has decided to transition to UX design. He accepted a position at a tech company in Seattle. The family will be moving to the Ballard neighborhood next month.
Marcus and the family have settled into their new home in Ballard, Seattle. He started his UX design role last week and is enjoying the change.
Marcus mentioned he had to stop running due to a knee injury. His doctor recommended low-impact exercise, so he's taken up yoga and swimming instead.
Marcus said he's been rethinking his diet after watching a documentary about ocean sustainability. He's transitioned to a pescatarian diet — he still eats fish but has stopped eating meat.
Marcus is enjoying his new UX design career. He says the architectural thinking translates well — spatial reasoning and user-centric design have a lot in common.
Marcus's son Liam just turned 13 and is interested in coding. Marcus is teaching him Python on weekends.
Marcus has started exploring the hiking trails around Seattle with the family. He says Mount Rainier is now his weekend goal. He also joined a local yoga studio in Ballard.
Marcus mentioned he's been cooking more fish-based meals at home. He's experimenting with Japanese recipes — sushi, miso soup, and grilled salmon have become staples.
Marcus reflected on his career change, saying it was the best decision he's made. He doesn't miss architecture as a profession but still appreciates it as a passion.
Marcus's daughter Mia is turning 9 next week. They're planning a birthday party at the Seattle Aquarium, which Mia loves.
